content.thinking:
The page is black - this is likely a JavaScript error crashing the app. The CSS defines a very dark background (`--bg-base: #09090f`) which would look black. The issue is likely a runtime JavaScript error.

Let me check the App.js - I notice something: `<AnalyticsTracker />` is placed OUTSIDE the `<Router>` context but it uses `useLocation` which requires being inside a Router. Actually wait, looking again - `AnalyticsTracker` is inside `<Router>` but it's a sibling of `<AuthProvider>`, not inside a `<Routes>`. That's actually valid since it's inside `<Router>`.
 
The completely black screen suggests JavaScript isn't loading or there's a fatal error, rather than just a styling issue. The "Reinicia para actualizar" message hints at a service worker or caching problem that might be preventing the app from loading properly.

Let me trace through the recent changes - the Instagram Carousel Generator was added recently, so I should check if that component or its integration into categories.js and CategoryPage is causing a render-time crash.

content.text: La pantalla en negro suele indicar un error JavaScript fatal. Veo en el navegador "Reinicia para actualizar" — puede ser caché o un crash en el render. Déjame revisar los cambios recientes.

diff --git a/src/data/categories.js b/src/data/categories.js
index 02eb8da..5fd1feb 100644
--- a/src/data/categories.js
+++ b/src/data/categories.js
@@ -694,6 +694,57 @@ Be specific, professional, and actionable. Every recommendation should be immedi
         buildUserMessage: (inputs) =>
           `Create a complete logo design system:\n\n**Brand Name:** ${inputs.brand_name}\n**Industry:** ${inputs.industry}\n**Logo Style:** ${inputs.style}\n**Preferred Colors:** ${inputs.colors || 'Open to suggestions'}\n**Brand Values / Personality:** ${inputs.values || 'Not specified'}\n**Avoid:** ${inputs.avoid || 'Nothing specific'}\n\nGenerate 3 distinct logo concepts with full color palettes, typography stacks, design guidelines and AI image prompts.`,
       },
+      {
+        id: 'instagram-carousel',
+        name: 'Instagram Carousel Generator',
+        description: 'Generate slide-by-slide carousel content with hooks, body copy and CTAs — upload a reference image for style matching',
+        icon: '🎠',
+        hasRefImage: true,
+        inputs: [
+          { id: 'topic', label: 'Carousel Topic / Hook', type: 'textarea', placeholder: 'e.g., 5 reasons why your Instagram reach is dying (and how to fix it)', required: true },
+          { id: 'slides', label: 'Number of Slides', type: 'select', options: ['3 slides', '5 slides', '7 slides', '10 slides'], required: true },
+          { id: 'niche', label: 'Niche / Industry', type: 'text', placeholder: 'e.g., personal finance, fitness, marketing, real estate', required: true },
+          { id: 'audience', label: 'Target Audience', type: 'text', placeholder: 'e.g., female entrepreneurs aged 25–35', required: true },
+          { id: 'tone', label: 'Tone', type: 'select', options: ['Educational', 'Inspirational', 'Conversational', 'Bold & Direct', 'Storytelling', 'Humorous'], required: true },
+          { id: 'cta', label: 'Call to Action (last slide)', type: 'text', placeholder: 'e.g., Save this post, Follow for more, DM me "CAROUSEL"', required: true },
+          { id: 'extra', label: 'Extra Context (optional)', type: 'textarea', placeholder: 'e.g., brand colors, key message, product to promote, specific stats to include' },
+        ],
+        systemPrompt: `You are an expert Instagram content strategist who creates viral carousel posts. Every carousel must stop the scroll, deliver real value, and drive saves and shares.
+
+Output each slide clearly labelled. For every slide include:
+- **Slide headline** (max 8 words — bold, punchy, curiosity-driven)
+- **Body copy** (2–4 short lines max — easy to read on mobile)
+- **Visual note** (1 line describing what to show: graphic, photo, icon, color background, etc.)
+
+Structure:
+**SLIDE 1 — COVER (Hook)**
+Make it impossible to scroll past. Use a bold claim, surprising stat, or direct question. Include the topic keyword.
+
+**SLIDE 2 to N-1 — VALUE SLIDES**
+Each slide = one clear point. Use numbered lists where possible. Short sentences. No fluff.
+
+**LAST SLIDE — CTA**
+Reinforce the main takeaway. Add the CTA naturally. Invite to save, share, or reply.
+
+---
+After all slides, add:
+
+**CAPTION**
+- Hook line (first 125 characters must hook — no emojis at the start)
+- 3–5 sentence body expanding on the carousel topic
+- CTA matching the last slide
+- 5 niche hashtags + 5 broad hashtags
+
+**COVER DESIGN TIPS**
+- Font suggestion
+- Background color / style recommendation
+- Layout tip
+
+Be specific to the niche and audience provided. No generic advice.`,
+        buildUserMessage: (inputs) =>
+          `Create a complete Instagram carousel:\n\n**Topic:** ${inputs.topic}\n**Slides:** ${inputs.slides}\n**Niche:** ${inputs.niche}\n**Target Audience:** ${inputs.audience}\n**Tone:** ${inputs.tone}\n**CTA:** ${inputs.cta}${inputs.extra ? `\n**Extra Context:** ${inputs.extra}` : ''}${inputs._ref_image_b64 ? '\n\n[Reference image provided — match the visual style, color palette and branding in the design tips.]' : ''}\n\nGenerate every slide fully. Include caption and design tips at the end.`,
+        maxTokens: 6000,
+      },
     ],
   },
   {

The diff looks syntactically fine. The issue might be something else. Let me check if the app is actually running and if there are build errors. Let me check if there's a package.json to understand how to run and build the app.

The black page could be because:
1. Firebase env variables are missing/wrong in production
2. A JavaScript runtime error 

Let me check if there's an env file and the CategoryPage to understand if `hasRefImage` is handled.

content.thinking:
The env vars are there. The `hasRefImage` flag is handled in AIToolInterface.js. Let me check if there's a build running or if the issue is with the deployment. 

Wait, looking at the .env output: `REACT_APP_API_URL=https://gormaran-growth-hub.onrender.com.env` - that looks wrong! The `.env` is appended to the URL. That's a potential issue.

Actually wait, let me re-read: the output shows:


```
REACT_APP_API_URL=https://gormaran-growth-hub.onrender.com.env
.env.example
```

That second line `.env.example` is from `ls .env*` — so the .env file shows `REACT_APP_API_URL=https://gormaran-growth-hub.onrender.com` and then `ls` output shows `.env` and `.env.example`. Actually I'm not sure. Let me look more carefully.

The output is confusing because the URL seems to end with `.env`, but that might just be how the output got concatenated. I need to figure out if the actual env variable is malformed or if it's just a display issue.

Since cat and ls are piped with `&&`, they should produce separate output. The REACT_APP_API_URL is probably correct as `https://gormaran-growth-hub.onrender.com`, and the `.env` and `.env.example` from ls just got merged in the display.

So the .env file exists with all the variables set. The black page in production suggests either the app needs to be rebuilt and redeployed after the categories.js changes, or there's a JavaScript error preventing the page from rendering.

Looking at the browser screenshot again, I see "gormaran.io" in the address bar and a "Reinicia para actualizar" button, which points to a service worker or PWA waiting for an update. The most likely issue is that a new build hasn't been deployed yet. Let me check the deployment setup and see if there's a build script and Firebase configuration.

content.thinking:
The build folder exists. The site shows a completely black page. This is likely because the build is outdated (before the recent changes were made, or there's a cached service worker issue), or there's a deployment issue.

Looking at the browser screenshot again:
- URL: gormaran.io
- "Reinicia para actualizar" button - this is a service worker update prompt, not a crash

This might be a service worker update available. The user needs to click "Reinicia para actualizar" (Restart to update) to get the new version.

But also - the page is completely black. 

I'm noticing the `_redirects` and `200.html` files in the build folder, which point to Netlify hosting with SPA routing. The black screen combined with the service worker update prompt suggests either an outdated cached version with a rendering issue, or the new build hasn't been deployed yet. The user should try clicking the restart button to see if that resolves it, but if not, a fresh build and deploy might be necessary.

content.thinking:
The build/index.html is there and has content (Facebook pixels, GTM, etc.). This is a pre-rendered page from react-snap.

The issue with the black screen is likely because:
1. react-snap pre-renders pages at build time (which bakes in the HTML)
2. The current build has react-snap output
3. But the actual deployment might need a new build

Looking at the screenshot again, the page shows `domain=localhost` in the Facebook pixel URL - this is the `build/index.html` on localhost! This suggests the pre-rendered HTML was built with `localhost` as the domain.
 
So react-snap is running during postbuild and pre-rendering pages with a headless browser pointed at localhost, which is standard. But the user is accessing gormaran.io in production, so the black page could mean either the new build hasn't deployed yet or a service worker is serving stale content. That "Reinicia para actualizar" button appears when there's a service worker update ready, but the black page persists even before clicking it, which suggests something else is going on.

The user just said their web doesn't work, and they might have deployed new code but it's showing black. The `categories.js` changes were only to source code, not necessarily rebuilt or redeployed, so they might need to trigger a full rebuild and redeploy. Or this could be a service worker caching issue on their browser. Given the `_redirects` file, they're likely using Netlify. I'm also noticing something odd in App.js where `<AnalyticsTracker />` is positioned inside `<Router>` but outside the `<AuthProvider>` and `<SubscriptionProvider>` wrappers, which could be causing issues.

Actually, looking at the component structure more carefully, `AnalyticsTracker` uses `useLocation()` and it's inside `<Router>`, so that should work fine. The black page is most likely because they need to rebuild and redeploy after the recent changes, or there's a Netlify deployment issue. I should ask them what they did last—did they just deploy, or did this break unexpectedly—and also check if a service worker is interfering.

content.thinking:
The `manifest.json` has `"display": "standalone"` - which makes this a PWA. The `"background_color": "#0f0f1a"` would show as the initial loading splash screen, which is basically black.

The "Reinicia para actualizar" button in Chrome suggests the service worker has an update ready. The black page could be because:
1. The service worker is intercepting requests and serving an old/broken cached version
2. The PWA update is needed

The user needs to:
1. Click "Reinicia para actualizar" to apply the service worker update
2. If that doesn't work, clear the site cache in Chrome DevTools


3. Check if they've deployed their code changes - if they only built locally without pushing to their host, the server is still serving the old version

I should ask what specifically changed to narrow down whether this is a caching issue or a deployment problem.

content.text:
Hay varias cosas a revisar. El botón "**Reinicia para actualizar**" es clave — hay un service worker con una actualización pendiente. Además el `manifest.json` tiene `display: standalone` (PWA), lo que puede causar pantalla negra durante la carga.

**Pasos en orden:**

**1. Haz clic en "Reinicia para actualizar"** en el navegador — es lo más probable que lo resuelva inmediatamente.

**2. Si sigue negro, fuerza una limpieza del service worker:**
- Abre Chrome DevTools (`F12` o `Cmd+Option+I`)
- Pestaña **Application** → **Service Workers**
- Clic en **Unregister**
- Luego **Ctrl+Shift+R** (hard refresh)

**3. Si sigue sin funcionar, verifica en qué estado está el deploy:**

¿Usas Netlify para el deploy? ¿Hiciste push de los últimos commits a la rama que monitorea Netlify? Los cambios de `categories.js` están commiteados pero si no has hecho deploy el sitio en producción sigue con la versión anterior.

¿Puedes decirme cómo tienes configurado el deploy (Netlify, manual, GitHub Actions)?

content.thinking:
Now I can see the errors clearly:

1. **`Uncaught TypeError: Cannot set properties of undefined (setting 'execStart')`** - from fbevents.js (Facebook Pixel)
2. **`Uncaught ReferenceError: fbq is not defined`** - from fbevents.js:20 (Facebook Pixel)
3. **`API KEY: AIzaSyA9kfWvJbm6ww9ZEa-PeHgfp6SvwU4BoI0`** - from config.js:5 (the console.log in firebase config - this is expected)


4. **`[Meta Pixel] - Duplicate Pixel ID: 956657160127097`** - warning
5. **`Uncaught (in promise) FirebaseError: Missing or insufficient permissions.`** - from AuthContext.js:91

The Facebook Pixel errors are from external scripts and shouldn't crash the app, but the unhandled promise rejection from the Firestore permissions error at AuthContext.js:91 is likely what's causing the black screen.

Let me check what's happening at that line in AuthContext.js to understand why the React app isn't mounting properly. I need to see the next thinking to rewrite it. Could you provide the next chunk of thinking that follows what you've shown me?

content.text:
El error clave es este:

> **`Uncaught (in promise) FirebaseError: Missing or insufficient permissions`** — `AuthContext.js:91`

React crasheó porque hay una excepción no capturada en el contexto de auth. Déjame ver ese archivo.

content.thinking:
Now I understand. Line 91 is `setLoading(false)`. But the error is "Missing or insufficient permissions" which comes from `createUserProfile` → `getDoc(userRef)` or `setDoc(userRef, ...)`.

The `createUserProfile` at line 87 is called with `await`, but if it throws (due to Firestore permissions), the error bubbles up and crashes the `onAuthStateChanged` callback. Since `setLoading(false)` at line 91 is only called AFTER `await createUserProfile(user)`, if `createUserProfile` throws, `setLoading` never gets called and `loading` stays `true` forever.


So the `!loading && children` check at line 110 keeps rendering nothing because loading never becomes false, which is why we're seeing the black screen. The real fix is wrapping `createUserProfile` in a try/catch block so that `setLoading(false)` always executes regardless of whether the profile creation succeeds or fails. The Firestore permissions issue is a separate problem, but the immediate black screen is caused by loading staying stuck at true.

content.text:
Encontré el bug exacto. En la línea 91, `setLoading(false)` solo se ejecuta si `createUserProfile` tiene éxito. Si Firestore lanza el error de permisos, la excepción se propaga, `setLoading` nunca se llama, `loading` se queda en `true` para siempre, y `{!loading && children}` nunca renderiza nada → pantalla negra.

La solución: mover `setLoading(false)` a un bloque `finally`.

content.text:
El fix está aplicado. Ahora necesitas **rebuild y redeploy** para que llegue a producción.

¿Cómo haces el deploy normalmente? (¿`npm run build` y subes a Netlify manualmente, o tienes CI/CD con GitHub?)

Aparte del fix del crash, el error de **"Missing or insufficient permissions"** de Firestore sigue existiendo — significa que las reglas de seguridad de tu base de datos están rechazando lecturas/escrituras. Eso puede causar que los usuarios no puedan loguearse correctamente aunque ya no veas pantalla negra. ¿Revisaste recientemente las reglas de Firestore en la consola de Firebase?

The user mentioned doing everything on Render, so maybe they're deploying the entire application there, including the frontend.

The most likely scenario is that Render is serving the React build from the Express server, or they're using Render's Static Sites feature. Either way, the fix just needs to be committed and pushed to GitHub, and Render will handle the auto-deployment from there.
